PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Continued To Jun-04-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mario Paolini (Counsel for Debra Dolch, Petitioner).

Mr. Paolini noted the history of the matter, the pendency of estate proceedings in Florida and that the essential purpose of the California proceeding was to liquidate California assests. With that explanation, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Special Administration, limiting the scope of the Special Administrator's authority to sale of the California real property with distribution to the Florida estate fiduciary of the proceeds of the sale. Bond was set at $680,000.00. The hearing on the Petition for Letters with Will Annexed was continued to June 4, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 with the understanding that the same may not be necessary if the Florida proceedings conclude by that date.

Judicial Officer: Dorothy McMath, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katie Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Jimmy Peralta; Court Examiner: None; Court Clerk: R. Michael Diles.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF SPECIAL ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Granted Per Written Order
